items.builder_takeaway: Directly relevant to auth decisions in CalenCall and ContractorVerify.
source_facts: JWTs are unnecessarily complex for most web authentication use cases; server-side sessions are simpler and more secure.
items.title: Stop Using JWTs

items.builder_takeaway: The Stop Killing Games initiative, which aimed to require publishers to maintain offline playability after service shutdown, failed to secure EU legislation despite 1.3M signatures.
source_facts: Stop Killing Games initiative failed to secure EU legislation despite 1.3M signatures.
items.title: Stop Killing Games fails to secure EU law despite 1.3M signatures

items.builder_takeaway: Directly affects anyone using Cursor for AI-assisted coding.
source_facts: SpaceX is acquiring Anysphere (the company behind Cursor AI coding assistant) for $60 billion.
items.title: SpaceX to buy Cursor (Anysphere) for $60B
items.uncertainty: Deal may not have closed; implications for existing Cursor users are unknown.

items.builder_takeaway: A 2019 blog post arguing that yak shaving is actually enjoyable and part of the creative process of software development.
source_facts: Yak shaving is an enjoyable and productive part of software development, not merely a distraction.

items.builder_takeaway: A 2022 Ars Technica retrospective on the PDP-11 minicomputer and its influence on Unix and modern computing architecture.
source_facts: The PDP-11 was among the most influential minicomputers in history, shaping Unix and modern computing architecture.
items.title: A brief tour of the PDP-11 (2022)

items.builder_takeaway: A home lab networking post about upgrading to 10Gb/s Ethernet using a Broadcom SFP+ module.

items.title: 10Gb/s Ethernet: switching to a Broadcom SFP+ module

items.builder_takeaway: Affects developer tooling and privacy workflows for anyone using Chrome.
source_facts: Chrome's next update completes Manifest V3, ending support for the blocking API used by popular ad blockers like uBlock Origin.
items.title: Google Chrome's next update will mark the end of popular ad blockers

items.builder_takeaway: Active attack pattern targeting developers via LinkedIn; directly actionable security awareness.
source_facts: A developer received a LinkedIn job offer containing a link to a backdoored coding challenge repository designed to compromise their system.
items.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
items.uncertainty: Details of exact payload not confirmed from content snippet.

items.builder_takeaway: Signal in the ongoing Rust-in-browsers trend; relevant to infrastructure and security tracking.
source_facts: Firefox is integrating zlib-rs, a Rust implementation of the zlib compression library, to replace its C implementation.
items.title: zlib-rs in Firefox

items.builder_takeaway: Useful calibration for evaluating Rust security claims in tech stack decisions.
source_facts: Rust eliminates traditional memory corruption CVEs but produces different types of memory safety bugs versus C/C++; the languages have different CVE profiles, not just different counts.
items.title: How memory safety CVEs differ between Rust and C/C++
items.uncertainty: Methodology details and dataset size not available from content snippet.

items.builder_takeaway: Directly relevant to Ollama usage decisions and when to use local vs. API models.
source_facts: Local LLMs have reached a quality level in 2026 where they are genuinely useful for daily development work without significant quality compromise versus cloud APIs.
items.title: Running local models is good now
items.uncertainty: Specific models and benchmarks cited in the article not available from content snippet.

items.builder_takeaway: Simon Willison released a Web Component that wraps GIF links to show a still frame with a play button, loading the full GIF only on click.
source_facts: A new Web Component wraps GIF links to show a static first frame with a play button, loading the full GIF only on demand.
items.title: <click-to-play> — a Web Component for on-demand GIF playback

items.builder_takeaway: Significant feature for datasette users; turns it into a usable admin UI.
source_facts: Datasette 1.0a34 adds row insert, edit, and delete capabilities directly in the web UI.
items.title: datasette 1.0a34 — row editing in the UI
items.uncertainty: Still alpha; API stability not guaranteed.

items.builder_takeaway: An experimental alpha plugin that integrates Tailscale with Datasette, serving a datasette instance directly on your Tailnet with a single command.
source_facts: datasette-tailscale 0.1a0 is an experimental plugin that runs datasette on a Tailscale network using a sidecar daemon.
items.title: datasette-tailscale 0.1a0
items.uncertainty: Very early alpha; may change significantly.

items.builder_takeaway: Strong signal from credible practitioner about local coding model quality; relevant to Ollama usage.
source_facts: Georgi Gerganov (llama.cpp creator) has been using Qwen3.6-27B daily for coding tasks and endorses it as a capable local coding model.
items.title: Georgi Gerganov endorses Qwen3.6-27B for local coding tasks
items.uncertainty: Tested on high-end hardware (M2 Ultra, RTX 5090); performance on more modest hardware may differ.

items.builder_takeaway: Claude Opus 4.8 experienced elevated errors on June 17, 2026 from 9:59pm to 10:41pm PT. Resolved. Covered by the larger multi-model incident.

items.builder_takeaway: Pattern of instability relevant to Claude API users building reliability-sensitive features.
source_facts: Claude API experienced three separate elevated-error incidents on June 16-17, 2026 affecting Sonnet and Opus models, with error rates reaching ~10% during the June 16 incident.
items.title: Claude API — elevated errors across Sonnet and Opus (Jun 16-17)
items.uncertainty: Root cause not disclosed in status updates.

items.builder_takeaway: Signal on how frontier labs approach model safety evaluation at scale.
source_facts: OpenAI introduced Deployment Simulation, a pre-release evaluation method using real historical conversation data to predict model behavior and safety properties before deployment.
items.title: OpenAI Deployment Simulation — predicting model behavior before release
items.uncertainty: Whether this changes existing model release timelines is unclear.
